title: HTTP/2 Frames, Streams, HPACK, Flow Control, and Go Boundaries · Program Defense
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for http/2 frames, streams, hpack, flow control, and go boundaries and requires the smallest strict HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.
Apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream.
Map pseudo-fields and regular fields, enforce ordering and connection-field rules, bound HPACK tables and header lists, and avoid indexing secrets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: H2 Preface Settings Frames · changed case · Quiz H2 Preface Settings Frames
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 4508 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.

hints: For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz H2 Go Transfer Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, H2 Go Transfer Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend one difficult decision · Quiz H2 Go Transfer Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz protocol team handles case 4515 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.

For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz H2 Go Transfer Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A training frame decoder should replace the maintained Go HTTP/2 stack in production.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using http and current plus specific case evidence.
